title: RepoBrief-Familienpublikation generationskohärent machen
goal: Make publication of multiple stable RepoBrief family manifests recoverable and generation-coherent so readers cannot observe an unintended mixture of old and new family generations after interruption or concurrent publication.

acceptance.assertion: All family manifests from one publication are bound to one explicit generation identity and readers have one documented rule for selecting a complete committed generation.
Injected failures before, between and after family publication cannot leave an unintended mixed generation as the authoritative read state; recovery is deterministic and tested.
Concurrent publishers are serialized or reconciled with a fail-closed protocol whose winner and rollback behavior are testable.
Existing single-family consumers continue to work or receive an explicit versioned migration path; consumer path validation remains fail-closed.
Focused crash/concurrency tests, the complete Lenskit suite, maintainability ratchets and real CLI dogfood pass on the exact implementation head.
The result does not claim distributed consensus, cross-host transactional filesystems, remote freshness or semantic truth.

validation: 43 focused generation, interruption, recovery, tamper and concurrency tests passed on the exact implementation head.
The complete local Lenskit suite passed on the exact committed head: 3986 passed, 1 skipped.
Ruff CI configuration, Ruff formatting, git diff --check and the graph maintainability ratchet passed; no new C901 violation was introduced and one baseline violation was resolved.
CLI dogfood published both families under generation 45e17f6a123dacca71c9e3618359ebf955b2d14fad29195ce323bcd54c8d2323 and independent readback selected that one complete generation.
Recovery dogfood reconstructed a deliberately removed stable compatibility projection without changing the authoritative generation.
All GitHub checks passed, including pytest-full, browser-tests, planning-registration, contracts, Ruff and CodeQL.

Fresh pre-merge readback confirmed unchanged base, head and GitHub patch; post-merge readback confirmed merge commit 58b44cd9261a07f67c1595d9ab87ce72a1b0b852 from reviewed head 480a4322a1b2d03cdc9a0645d65ceb5a7386d3de.
metadata.outcome: Lenskit PR #1010 now publishes all requested RepoBrief/Lenskit family manifests as one immutable, descriptor-bound generation selected by one atomic pointer. The reader verifies the pointer, descriptor, source bundle and every family fail-closed; per-lane locking starts before materialization; historical stable family paths remain non-authoritative compatibility projections with deterministic recovery. Hostile ancestor-directory replacement resistance and retention/garbage collection remain explicit follow-ups.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-generation-contract: All requested families carry one deterministic generation ID and one shared generation timestamp. One atomically replaced pointer selects the complete descriptor-bound generation, and the reader validates the documented read-once selection rule.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-interruption-recovery: Injected failures between family writes and at pointer replacement leave the previous pointer authoritative. Failures after pointer commit degrade only non-authoritative compatibility projections, which deterministic recovery rebuilds from the selected generation.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-concurrency: An exclusive repository/ref lane flock serializes publishers before bundle materialization. Focused tests cover both different and identical concurrent bundles and verify a complete deterministic winner.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-compatibility: Historical single-family stable paths remain available and explicitly record that they are non-authoritative compatibility projections. Cross-family consumers receive a versioned pointer-and-descriptor migration rule; traversal, symlink and integrity mismatches fail closed.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-validation: 43 focused tests, the complete 3986-test Lenskit suite, Ruff, formatting, diff checks, maintainability ratchets, CLI publication/recovery dogfood and all GitHub checks passed on head 480a4322a1b2d03cdc9a0645d65ceb5a7386d3de.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t024-nonclaims: No distributed consensus, cross-host transactional filesystem, remote freshness, semantic truth or hostile ancestor-directory replacement protection is claimed; RBV1-T025 remains the path-hardening follow-up.
